signals.title: ⚠️ High Materiality Anomaly: Mumbai revenue 82% below 4-week average
signals.details: Week of 2025-02-24: Mumbai bookings totalled ₹4,038 against a trailing 4-week average of ₹22,161. Statistical confidence: 3.1σ deviation. Isolated to 2 orders from enterprise accounts (customer_id C002, C004). Remaining regions (Delhi, Bangalore, Chennai) within normal variance. Estimated revenue at risk: ₹18,123 vs baseline.

investigation.root_cause: Two Mumbai enterprise accounts placed unusually small orders in week 8 (Feb 24). Historical pattern shows these accounts consistently drive ₹8,000–12,000 each per week. This week both accounts dropped to under ₹1,200 combined — consistent with a procurement freeze or contract renewal gap rather than a product or pricing issue.
investigation.recommended_action: Trigger account health check for C002 and C004. Review renewal dates. Consider proactive outreach from the enterprise success team before end of Q1.

signals.title: Data quality: 1 customer record has null email — downstream comms at risk
signals.details: Customer C003 has no email on record. This account placed 97 orders totalling ₹89,340 in the trailing 12 months. Without an email address, automated renewal reminders and invoice dispatch will silently fail.

signals.title: Monitoring note: mixed date formats detected in orders.created_at
signals.details: 341 of 507 order records use MM/DD/YYYY format while 166 use ISO 8601 (YYYY-MM-DD). No revenue impact, but time-series aggregations that rely on date parsing will produce inconsistent results until normalised.
